script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'map-edu-platform-architecture',
  description: 'Faithfully map the current lesson/test/roadmap/KTP/content architecture of the edu platform, then adversarially verify the load-bearing claims',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Map', detail: 'parallel readers over content, KTP/roadmap, lessons, tests, progress, docs' },
    { title: 'Verify', detail: 'adversarial verification of the most load-bearing architectural claims' },
  ],
}

const MAP_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  additionalProperties: false,
  required: ['subsystem', 'entities', 'flows', 'reuse', 'aiCost', 'problems', 'evidence'],
  properties: {
    subsystem: { type: 'string' },
    entities: {
      type: 'array',
      description: 'Each data model / collection / major type in this subsystem',
      items: {
        type: 'object',
        additionalProperties: false,
        required: ['name', 'file', 'fields', 'relationships', 'notes'],
        properties: {
          name: { type: 'string' },
          file: { type: 'string', description: 'file:line' },
          fields: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'field: type — include enums, refs, embedded vs referenced' },
          relationships: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'how it links to other entities (ref, embed, denormalized id, etc.)' },
          notes: { type: 'string', description: 'indexes, uniqueness, embedding decisions, anything notable' },
        },
      },
    },
    flows: {
      type: 'array',
      description: 'Generation / read / write flows: how things are produced and stored',
      items: {
        type: 'object',
        additionalProperties: false,
        required: ['name', 'steps', 'aiCalls'],
        properties: {
          name: { type: 'string' },
          steps: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'ordered steps with file:line where the work happens' },
          aiCalls: { type: 'string', description: 'which steps call an LLM, what model/prompt shape, what is cached vs regenerated' },
        },
      },
    },
    reuse: { type: 'string', description: 'What content is reused/cached vs regenerated from scratch each time, and the cache key/mechanism if any' },
    aiCost: { type: 'string', description: 'Where AI generation cost is incurred, per what unit (per user? per node? per attempt?), and whether results are shared across users' },
    problems: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'Smells, duplication, scalability/cost risks, coupling, missing abstractions — be specific with file:line' },
    evidence: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'Key verbatim code snippets or schema excerpts with file:line that a skeptic could re-check' },
  },
}

phase('Map')

const readers = [
  {
    label: 'content-model',
    prompt: `You are mapping the CONTENT HIERARCHY subsystem of an educational platform (Node/TS + MongoDB/Mongoose) at /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP.
Read fully and carefully:
- server/src/models/Subject.model.ts
- server/src/services/subjectContent.service.ts
- server/src/controllers/subject.controller.ts
- server/src/routes/subject.routes.ts
Skim subject.json at repo root as an example import.
Goal: document the content hierarchy. Specifically answer:
- Exact schema of Subject and any nested entities (Book, Chapter, Topic, Paragraph). Are books/chapters/topics/paragraphs EMBEDDED subdocuments or SEPARATE collections? What are the id fields, order fields, content fields?
- Does a Subject support MULTIPLE books today? How is ordering of books/chapters/topics/paragraphs handled?
- Is there any notion of "subtopic" (подтема) as a distinct entity, or only topic/paragraph?
- How is content validated on import? Any uniqueness constraints?
Return the structured map. Use exact field names and file:line. Quote the Mongoose schema fields verbatim in evidence.`,
  },
  {
    label: 'ktp-roadmap',
    prompt: `You are mapping the KTP (календарно-тематический план) + ROADMAP subsystem of an educational platform at /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP.
Read fully and carefully:
- server/src/models/KtpCatalog.model.ts
- server/src/models/CanonicalRoadmap.model.ts
- server/src/services/ktp.service.ts
- server/src/services/roadmap.ai.service.ts
- server/src/roadmap/roadmap.rules.ts
- server/src/controllers/roadmap.controller.ts
And read server/src/services/roadmap.service.ts (it is ~1080 lines — read it in full across multiple reads if needed; this is the core file).
Goal: explain how the learning roadmap is constructed. Specifically:
- What is the KtpCatalog — a reference/справочник of КТП topics? What are its fields and granularity (subject? grade? topic list?)
- What is CanonicalRoadmap vs the live roadmap? Are roadmap NODES persisted entities or computed on the fly?
- How are roadmap nodes derived: from KTP catalog (live) or from book topics? (Memory hint: roadmap is built from the KTP reference live, not from linear book topics — verify or refute this against code.)
- What are roadmap NODES, EDGES, prerequisites/unlock rules? Where do they live?
- How does a roadmap node link back to content (topics/paragraphs/books) and to lessons and tests?
Return the structured map with exact field names and file:line. In evidence, quote the node/edge schema and the function that builds the roadmap.`,
  },
  {
    label: 'lesson-gen',
    prompt: `You are mapping the LESSON GENERATION subsystem of an educational platform at /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP.
Read fully and carefully:
- server/src/models/NodeLessonContent.model.ts
- server/src/services/nodeLessonContent.service.ts
- server/src/services/roadmapLesson.service.ts
Goal: explain how a lesson for a roadmap node is generated and whether it is reused. Specifically:
- What triggers lesson generation? Is a lesson generated per-node-globally (shared across all students) or per-user?
- What is the cache key for NodeLessonContent (subject? node? book? version?)? Is there versioning/invalidation?
- What does the AI prompt for a lesson look like — what source material is fed in (paragraph text? topic title? KTP?)?
- Is the lesson regenerated from scratch each time or fetched if it exists?
- How does lesson content relate to test generation downstream?
Return the structured map with exact field names and file:line. Quote the cache-lookup logic and the model schema in evidence.`,
  },
  {
    label: 'test-gen',
    prompt: `You are mapping the TEST / QUESTION GENERATION subsystem of an educational platform at /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP.
Read fully and carefully:
- server/src/models/Test.model.ts
- server/src/services/ai.service.ts (~556 lines, read fully)
- server/src/controllers/test.controller.ts (~25k chars, read fully)
- server/src/services/testResult.service.ts
Goal: explain how tests/questions are generated, stored, validated, and whether questions are reused. Specifically:
- Exact Test schema: what is a question? (text, options, correct answer, explanation, difficulty, links to topic/paragraph/node?)
- Where do questions come from? What source material is fed to the LLM? Which model/prompt?
- Is there ANY question bank / reuse of previously generated questions? Or is every test generated fresh per attempt? (This is critical — verify.)
- How is the generated test tied to a roadmap node / topic / KTP? How is "coverage of the topic" ensured, if at all?
- What anti-hallucination / grounding measures exist (e.g. feeding source text, schema validation, answer verification)?
- How are answers checked and results analyzed?
Return the structured map with exact field names and file:line. In evidence quote the Test schema, the generation prompt, and any persistence/reuse logic.`,
  },
  {
    label: 'progress-attempts',
    prompt: `You are mapping the PROGRESS / ATTEMPTS / MASTERY subsystem of an educational platform at /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP.
Read fully and carefully:
- server/src/models/UserRoadmapProgress.model.ts
- server/src/models/RoadmapAttempt.model.ts
- server/src/models/Test.model.ts (the attempt-relevant parts)
- server/src/services/testResult.service.ts
- server/src/services/profileStats.service.ts
- server/src/services/trial.service.ts
Goal: explain how a student's progress, mastery, and unlocking of roadmap nodes work. Specifically:
- How is per-user progress stored? Per node? What states (locked/available/done/mastery score)?
- How does completing a test update progress and unlock the next node?
- Is mastery tracked at node level only, or at topic/subtopic/knowledge level?
- What is "trial" — a trial mode? How does it differ from the full roadmap flow?
Return the structured map with exact field names and file:line. Quote progress schema and the unlock logic in evidence.`,
  },
  {
    label: 'docs-intent',
    prompt: `You are extracting the DOCUMENTED ARCHITECTURE & INTENT of an educational platform at /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP, to compare documented design against code reality.
Read fully:
- docs/ARCHITECTURE.md
- docs/ROADMAP_SPEC.md
- docs/NOTION_DOCS.md
- README.md (skim the architecture-relevant parts)
Goal: capture how the system is DESIGNED to work according to docs, especially: content model (subject/book/chapter/topic/paragraph), roadmap/KTP, lesson generation, test generation, any mention of a question bank, knowledge blocks, subtopics, or reuse strategy. Note any place where the docs describe an intent that may not be (or may be) implemented.
Return the structured map. For 'entities' list the documented entities. For 'flows' list documented generation flows. In 'problems' list documented-but-questionable design decisions or gaps the docs themselves admit. In evidence quote the most architecturally important doc passages with file:line.`,
  },
]

const maps = (await parallel(
  readers.map(r => () => agent(r.prompt, { label: r.label, phase: 'Map', schema: MAP_SCHEMA }))
)).filter(Boolean)

phase('Verify')

// Load-bearing claims that my architecture recommendation will rest on. Each verifier must
// independently re-read the code and try to REFUTE the claim, citing file:line.
const VERDICT_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  additionalProperties: false,
  required: ['claim', 'verdict', 'evidence', 'nuance'],
  properties: {
    claim: { type: 'string' },
    verdict: { type: 'string', enum: ['CONFIRMED', 'REFUTED', 'PARTIAL'] },
    evidence: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'file:line citations and verbatim snippets supporting the verdict' },
    nuance: { type: 'string', description: 'caveats, exceptions, partial truths — what a careful architect must know' },
  },
}

const claims = [
  {
    label: 'verify:no-question-bank',
    claim: 'There is NO persistent question bank: every test is generated fresh by the LLM per attempt/request, and previously generated questions are not reused across users or attempts.',
    files: 'server/src/models/Test.model.ts, server/src/services/ai.service.ts, server/src/controllers/test.controller.ts, server/src/services/testResult.service.ts',
  },
  {
    label: 'verify:lesson-reuse',
    claim: 'Lesson content IS cached and reused per roadmap node (shared across users) via NodeLessonContent — it is NOT regenerated per user each time.',
    files: 'server/src/models/NodeLessonContent.model.ts, server/src/services/nodeLessonContent.service.ts, server/src/services/roadmapLesson.service.ts',
  },
  {
    label: 'verify:roadmap-from-ktp',
    claim: 'The roadmap is built live from the KTP catalog (KtpCatalog) reference, NOT from the linear topic list of books; roadmap nodes are derived from KTP entries.',
    files: 'server/src/models/KtpCatalog.model.ts, server/src/models/CanonicalRoadmap.model.ts, server/src/services/ktp.service.ts, server/src/services/roadmap.service.ts, server/src/services/roadmap.ai.service.ts',
  },
  {
    label: 'verify:multi-book-and-subtopics',
    claim: 'A Subject can contain MULTIPLE books, and content is modeled as embedded hierarchy subject>book>chapter>topic>paragraph with NO distinct "subtopic" (подтема) entity between topic and paragraph.',
    files: 'server/src/models/Subject.model.ts, server/src/services/subjectContent.service.ts, server/src/controllers/subject.controller.ts',
  },
  {
    label: 'verify:test-node-grounding',
    claim: 'Test/question generation is grounded in node/topic content (source text is fed to the LLM) and there is some attempt to ensure topic coverage; OR conversely there is little grounding and coverage is not enforced — determine which is true.',
    files: 'server/src/services/ai.service.ts, server/src/services/roadmapLesson.service.ts, server/src/controllers/test.controller.ts',
  },
]

const verdicts = (await parallel(
  claims.map(c => () => agent(
    `Adversarially VERIFY this architectural claim about the codebase at /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP. Re-read the relevant code yourself; do NOT trust prior summaries. Try hard to REFUTE the claim — only return CONFIRMED if the code clearly supports it. Cite file:line for every assertion.

CLAIM: ${c.claim}

Start by reading: ${c.files}

Return your verdict.`,
    { label: c.label, phase: 'Verify', schema: VERDICT_SCHEMA }
  ))
)).filter(Boolean)

return { maps, verdicts }

maps.subsystem: Content Hierarchy (Subject → Book → Chapter → Topic → Paragraph → Content)

entities.name: Subject (root collection)
fields: title: String — required, trim, unique, minlength 1, maxlength 200, index
relationships: EMBEDS books as subdocument array (books: [BookSchema], line 150)

Referenced BY other collections via subjectId (Test, CanonicalRoadmap, KtpCatalog, NodeLessonContent, UserRoadmapProgress, RoadmapAttempt, RoadmapChatAttachment, ProfileSubjectPair, User.testHistory) — see deleteSubject cascade subject.controller.ts:288-315
Topic.ktpTopicIds reference KtpCatalog.topics._id (cross-collection M:N, NOT a nested ref)
entities.notes: Stored in collection 'subjects' (line 153). title is the ONLY uniqueness constraint (unique:true line 133). Three extra indexes on embedded ids: 'books._id', 'books.chapters._id', 'books.chapters.topics._id' (lines 158-160). Instance methods getBookContent/getChapterContent/getTopicsContent concatenate paragraph text (lines 168-212).

entities.name: Book (embedded subdocument)
fields: _id: ObjectId (auto — BookSchema does NOT set _id:false, so each book gets its own _id)
title: String — required, trim, minlength 1, maxlength 300
contentLanguage: String — optional, trim, maxlength 80 (language for AI generation)
relationships: EMBEDDED inside Subject.books
EMBEDS chapters: [ChapterSchema] (line 116)
entities.notes: NO 'order' field on Book — book ordering is implicit by array position only. timestamps:false. _id present (used everywhere via b._id?.toString()).

entities.name: Chapter (embedded subdocument)
fields: title: String — required, trim, minlength 1, maxlength 200
relationships: EMBEDDED inside Book.chapters
EMBEDS topics: [TopicSchema] (line 95)
entities.notes: order is required (line 90-94), unlike Topic.order. Server assigns order on add (addChapter: order = book.chapters.length, controller:201) and reorder sets order=index. timestamps:false.

entities.name: Topic (embedded subdocument)
fields: title: String — required, trim, minlength 1, maxlength 200
order: Number — OPTIONAL, min 0 (optional for legacy data; line 72-76)
ktpTopicIds: [ObjectId] — array of KtpCatalog.topics._id, M:N mapping to КТП (line 77-78)
relationships: EMBEDDED inside Chapter.topics
EMBEDS paragraphs: [ParagraphSchema] (line 79)
ktpTopicIds: loose ObjectId refs to KtpCatalog.topics._id (validated against KtpCatalog in setTopicKtp, controller:467-474)
entities.notes: This is the deepest titled entity below chapter. There is NO subtopic/подтема entity. ktpTopicIds is a bare [{type: ObjectId}] with no ref/populate. order optional, set on add (controller:218) and reorder.

entities.name: Paragraph (embedded subdocument)
relationships: EMBEDDED inside Topic.paragraphs
EMBEDS content: ContentSchema (required, line 58-61)
entities.notes: Holds the actual text payload via content. order required. timestamps:false.

entities.name: Content (embedded, _id:false)
fields: text: String — required, trim, minlength 1 (the actual study text)
pages: [Number] — each required (page numbers in source book)
relationships: EMBEDDED inside Paragraph.content
EMBEDS metadata: MetadataSchema (required)
entities.notes: _id:false (line 50) — no own id. text is what feeds AI test/roadmap generation.

entities.name: Metadata (embedded, _id:false)
fields: difficulty: String — enum Difficulty ['easy','medium','hard'], NOT required
relationships: EMBEDDED inside Content.metadata
entities.notes: _id:false (line 33). All fields effectively optional (difficulty required:false).

flows.name: Import whole subject (POST /subjects/import)
steps: Route validates only title (1..200) and updateIfExists boolean — subject.routes.ts:47-59. NO deep body validation of books/chapters/topics/paragraphs here.
Controller importSubject reads {title,description,books} from body — subject.controller.ts:58-59
If existing subject with same title and !updateIfExists → 400 error (controller:64-68)
If existing && updateIfExists → Subject.updateOne $set description/subjectKind/books, wholesale REPLACING books array (controller:69-81)
Else Subject.create({title, description, subjectKind, books: books||[]}) — controller:114-119. Mongoose schema validation runs on create (required text, enums, lengths).
Computes stats (book/chapter/topic/paragraph counts) by reducing nested arrays (controller:121-137)
flows.aiCalls: None. Pure DB write. AI consumes this content later via resolveBookContentForAI.

flows.name: Import single book (POST /subjects/:subjectId/books/import)
steps: Route DOES deep-validate the book shape with express-validator wildcards: book.title, book.chapters[*].title, .topics (isArray), .topics[*].title, .topics[*].paragraphs (isArray), .paragraphs[*].content.text required — subject.routes.ts:124-140
Controller importBook pushes one book onto subject.books without touching others, then subject.save() — controller:172-188 (Mongoose validation runs on save)
flows.aiCalls: None.

flows.name: Granular add (addBook/addChapter/addTopic/addParagraph)
steps: addBook: subject.books.push, save — controller:158-169 (book has no order)
addChapter: finds book, push {title, order: book.chapters.length, topics:[]} — server assigns order to end to avoid collisions (controller:191-204)
addTopic: finds book→chapter, push {title, order: chapter.topics.length, paragraphs:[]} — controller:207-221
addParagraph: finds book→chapter→topic, push {order, content} — controller:224-242. subjectId/bookId/chapterId passed via QUERY params, not path.
flows.aiCalls: None.

flows.name: Reorder (drag-and-drop)
steps: reorderChapters/reorderTopics/reorderParagraphs receive ordered id arrays — controller:398-449
assertPermutation guards that posted ids are an exact permutation of existing ids (no missing/dup/foreign) — controller:30-36
Sets each element's order = its index, then save — chapters/topics/paragraphs all use the numeric 'order' field for sort
flows.aiCalls: None.

flows.name: Read content for AI (resolveBookContentForAI)
steps: Subject.findById(dto.subjectId) — subjectContent.service.ts:15
Find book by _id in subject.books (in-memory .find) — service:18
If topicFocus given (no chapterId, not fullBook) → fuzzy-match a chapter by title substring — service:26-34
fullBook or no chapter → subject.getBookContent(bookId) joins all paragraph.content.text with '\n\n' — service:37, model:168-177
Else getChapterContent(bookId, chapterId) joins that chapter's paragraph texts — service:43, model:183-194
Builds IContentForAI {text, metadata{subjectTitle,bookTitle,chapterTitle,topics,bookAuthor,contentLanguage,chapterTitles}} — service:56-68. chapterTitles sorted by chapter.order (service:52-54).
flows.aiCalls: This is the READ path that produces the text payload fed to downstream LLM test/roadmap generation (callers, not in these files). No LLM call here itself; getTopicsContent (model:200-212) supports topic-subset reads.

maps.reuse: Content is authored once (import or granular add) and stored as embedded subdocuments inside the single Subject document; it is re-read in full for every AI generation via getBookContent/getChapterContent/getTopicsContent (concatenated text). There is NO caching layer in these files — the AI-ready text (IContentForAI.text) is rebuilt from scratch on every resolveBookContentForAI call by re-joining all paragraph texts. The deliberate design choice (model comment lines 17-20) is embedding-over-references so the whole content tree loads in one Subject.findById query rather than caching. The only persisted derived/reused artifact is Topic.ktpTopicIds (the book-topic→КТП-topic M:N mapping), which is computed by curators and stored, not regenerated.
maps.aiCost: No LLM calls occur in this subsystem. It is the content store/read layer that FEEDS AI generation elsewhere. The relevant cost driver it controls is granularity: resolveBookContentForAI returns either the full book text (dto.fullBook or no chapter → getBookContent, every paragraph in the book) or a single chapter's text (getChapterContent). Whole-book selection sends the entire embedded corpus to the LLM, so cost scales with chosen scope (book vs chapter vs topic-subset). Output is per-request and not shared/cached across users — each generation re-concatenates and re-sends the same source text.
problems: MULTIPLE books per Subject ARE supported structurally (books: [BookSchema], unbounded array) and the granular/import APIs append books, but Book has NO 'order' field — book ordering is purely implicit array insertion order, inconsistent with Chapter (required order), Topic (optional order) and Paragraph (required order). subject.json example also has only one book.

Inconsistent ordering model: Chapter.order required (model:90), Paragraph.order required (model:53), Topic.order OPTIONAL (model:73, legacy data), Book has no order at all. Sorting relies on order where present; getBookContent (model:172-176) iterates chapters/topics/paragraphs in stored ARRAY order, NOT by .order, so a stale array order vs order field can desync AI text sequence. Only chapterTitles metadata is explicitly sorted by order (service:52-54).

No uniqueness anywhere except Subject.title (model:133). Books, chapters, topics, paragraphs can be exact duplicates (same title/order) with no constraint — and addChapter/addTopic assign order=array length, so deleting from the middle then adding can produce duplicate order values until a reorder runs.

updateIfExists import REPLACES the entire books array wholesale via $set (controller:72-81). Since all chapter/topic/paragraph _ids are auto-regenerated on re-import, this orphans every external reference keyed on those embedded ids — notably Topic.ktpTopicIds mappings, KtpCatalog links, roadmap/NodeLessonContent that may target old ids. No id preservation on re-import.

Validation asymmetry between the two import paths: /subjects/import does NOT deep-validate book/chapter/topic/paragraph shape at the route (subject.routes.ts:47-59) — relies solely on Mongoose schema validation on create; whereas /subjects/:subjectId/books/import DOES (routes:124-140). content.metadata is schema-required (model:46-49) but subject.json paragraphs omit fields and one even omits difficulty — fine since difficulty optional, but a metadata object is mandatory.

Unbounded document growth: entire subject (all books/chapters/topics/paragraphs/full text) is one MongoDB document. With many books or large texts this risks the 16MB BSON document limit and forces loading the whole tree on every Subject.findById/getAllSubjects (getAllSubjects even selects 'books', controller:145-147, returning full content trees for listing).

ktpTopicIds is a bare [{type: Schema.Types.ObjectId}] with no ref and no schema-level validation that ids exist; validity is enforced only imperatively in setTopicKtp against KtpCatalog (controller:467-474). Direct import of a book with ktpTopicIds would bypass that check.

getBookContent/getChapterContent return '' on missing book/chapter (model:170,184-188) and resolveBookContentForAI then throws badRequest('No content available') (service:48-50) — a missing-vs-empty distinction is lost, so a wrong bookId can surface as a generic content error in some paths.
evidence: BookSchema (model:98-117) has NO order field: `const BookSchema = new Schema<IBook>({ title: {...maxlength:300}, author: {...}, contentLanguage: {...maxlength:80}, chapters: [ChapterSchema] }, { timestamps: false });`
ChapterSchema order REQUIRED (model:90-94): `order: { type: Number, required: true, min: 0 }`

TopicSchema order OPTIONAL + ktpTopicIds (model:72-79): `/** Порядок темы в главе (drag-and-drop). Необязателен для старых данных. */ order: { type: Number, min: 0 }, /** Темы КТП ... M:N. */ ktpTopicIds: [{ type: Schema.Types.ObjectId }], paragraphs: [ParagraphSchema]`
Embedding is deliberate (model:17-20): `Оптимизация: Использую вложенные схемы (embedded documents) вместо ссылок, так как контент всегда читается целиком для генерации тестов.`

Subject embeds books, only title unique (model:128-154): `title: { type: String, required: true, trim: true, unique: true, minlength: 1, maxlength: 200, index: true }` ... `books: [BookSchema]` ... `{ timestamps: true, collection: 'subjects' }`

ParagraphSchema (model:52-62): `order: { type: Number, required: true, min: 0 }, content: { type: ContentSchema, required: true }` — and ContentSchema (model:35-50) `text: { required: true, trim: true, minlength: 1 }, pages: [{ type: Number, required: true }], metadata: { type: MetadataSchema, required: true }`, both with `{ _id: false }`.

getBookContent iterates by array order, not .order (model:172-176): `return book.chapters.flatMap((chapter)=>chapter.topics).flatMap((topic)=>topic.paragraphs).map((paragraph)=>paragraph.content.text).join('\n\n');`
updateIfExists wholesale replace (controller:72-81): `await Subject.updateOne({ _id: existing._id }, { $set: { description: ..., subjectKind, books: booksArr } });`
Server-assigned order on add (controller:201): `book.chapters.push({ title, order: book.chapters.length, topics: [] });`

No subtopic entity anywhere: grep for 'subtopic|подтем|Subtopic|subTopic' across server/src returns only one hit in roadmap.ai.service.ts:396 (an LLM prompt instruction 'раздели на 2–4 ... урока по подтемам'), not a data model.
subject.json example has exactly ONE book object under `books` with chapters[].order = 1,2,3 and topics WITHOUT an order field (topics rely on array position).

maps.subsystem: KTP (календарно-тематический план) + ROADMAP — learning roadmap construction

entities.name: KtpCatalog (КТП справочник)
fields: subjectId: ObjectId ref Subject — required, index, UNIQUE (one catalog per subject)
year?: Number (2000..2100) — год актуальности справочника ЦТ
relationships: 1:1 with Subject (unique subjectId)
topics are EMBEDDED subdocuments (not referenced)
each embedded topic._id is the stable, rename-safe id referenced by Subject.Topic.ktpTopicIds[] and by roadmap nodeId 'ktp:{topicId}'
entities.notes: This IS the reference/справочник of КТП topics. Granularity: per-subject, per-year, an ORDERED list of topics. NOT per-grade/class — grade info lives in book topics that map onto a КТП topic. The catalog is the canonical ordered topic list 'set by the testing center'.

entities.name: IKtpTopic (embedded КТП topic)
fields: _id: ObjectId — STABLE id (the rename-safe anchor; { _id: true })
order: Number — required, min 0 (defines linear sequence)
code?: String — max 80 (testing-center code, display/import only, NOT a stable id)
prerequisiteKtpTopicIds?: [ObjectId] — explicit prereqs (declared but UNUSED; default is linear order by `order`)
relationships: embedded in KtpCatalog.topics
_id targeted by Subject chapter topic.ktpTopicIds[] (the mapping link from book topic -> КТП topic)
entities.notes: Granularity = single topic of a subject. prerequisiteKtpTopicIds is a placeholder ('задел') — buildKtpCanonicalNodes ignores it and uses linear order.

entities.name: CanonicalRoadmap

maps.reuse: Live КТП roadmap nodes are REGENERATED on the fly on every resolveCanonical call (no persisted node store for the КТП path). 'Caching' is effectively the КТП catalog + Subject.book-topic mappings themselves. Per-user progress (UserRoadmapProgress) is the only persisted overlay and is re-merged against freshly built nodes each read (mergeProgressWithCanonical), with canonicalVersion = floor(max(subject.updatedAt, ktp.updatedAt)/1000) acting as a change/version key. Roadmap version is time-derived, not a counter, so any edit to the subject or КТП bumps it. AI-consolidated lesson TEXT is cached in node_lesson_content keyed by (ktpTopicId, sourceHash) — invalidated when mapped source texts change. AI personal insights (coachSummary/hints) and the fallback CanonicalRoadmap doc are the only AI-generated content; the fallback doc is deleted whenever a КТП rebuild runs. testId per node is re-queried (latest Test by bookId+chapterId) on every getCanonical/enrichPersonalNodeView — not cached.
maps.aiCost: AI cost is incurred at: (1) admin canonical generation per subject (generateCanonicalFromBookContent, one gpt-4o-mini call per admin rebuild — rare, shared across all users of that subject); (2) personal enrichment per request when ?ai=1 (enrichPersonalRoadmap, one gpt-4o-mini call PER USER PER getPersonal/getNext request — NOT cached, recomputed every call); (3) lazy lesson consolidation per КТП node (consolidateLessonContent, cached in node_lesson_content by sourceHash, so shared across users and regenerated only when sources change); (4) per-node lesson chat and lesson summary (chatLessonNode/generateLessonSummary, per user/per message; summary cached on canonical metadata.lesson.summary). The deterministic roadmap graph, mastery, unlock and recommendation logic cost NO AI.
problems: N+1 DB queries in getCanonical (roadmap.service.ts:194-229) and getPersonalSnapshot/enrichPersonalNodeView (ts:258-268, ts:398-403): a separate Test.findOne per node to resolve testId — scales O(nodes) queries per request; getHomeOverview (ts:588-624) and getPickerSubjects loop calls getPersonalSnapshot per subject, multiplying this.

Roadmap version is derived from floor(max(updatedAt)/1000) (ts:128, ts:1071-1074) — two edits within the same second collide on the same version; also couples version to wall-clock, making it non-monotonic if updatedAt is ever set backwards.

getPersonalSnapshot performs WRITES on a GET path (upsert/re-merge UserRoadmapProgress ts:298-317) — read endpoint has side effects and can race under concurrent requests; getHomeOverview/getPickerSubjects trigger these writes per subject.
Duplicated metadata-extraction + testId/chapterUrl logic exists twice: getCanonical (ts:196-227) and enrichPersonalNodeView (ts:251-281) — should be one helper.

Repeated boilerplate across recordTestSubmitted/markLessonRead/markLessonComplete/acknowledgeMaterialMastery/applyTrialKtpResults: same merge -> filter-out -> push -> mergeProgressWithCanonical -> findOneAndUpdate pattern (ts:759-770, 849-863, 895-909, 978-989, 1017-1031) — ripe for a single updateNodeProgress(nodeId, mutator) abstraction.

markLessonRead exists on both roadmapService (ts:878) and (per controller) roadmapLessonService — two lesson-read paths; node.lessonReadAt is set by both markLessonRead and markLessonComplete (last-lesson) which can conflict.

prerequisiteKtpTopicIds on IKtpTopic (KtpCatalog.model.ts:24) is declared but never read — buildKtpCanonicalNodes hardcodes a purely LINEAR prev->next chain (roadmapKtp.util.ts:101), so the DAG is always a path; the cycle-check (roadmapGraph.ts) and AND-prereq machinery are over-engineered for the live path.

maps.reuse: Lesson content IS reused/shared globally, not per-user. The NodeLessonContent document is keyed by (subjectId, ktpTopicId) with a unique compound index (NodeLessonContent.model.ts:36) and has no userId — so the AI-consolidated lesson is generated once and served to every student of that subject. Cache key for content version = sourceHash, a sha256 over the mapped source topics' [topicId,text] pairs (nodeLessonContent.service.ts:50-52). Invalidation is by content change only: on read, a stored row is reused if generatedBy==='manual' (never invalidated) OR sourceHash===freshly-computed-hash; otherwise it regenerates and upserts the same row (nodeLessonContent.service.ts:107-110, 138-157). There is no numeric version field on NodeLessonContent and no explicit cache-bust endpoint — editing/remapping the source textbook topics changes the hash and triggers regeneration on the next request. Per-lesson summaries are cached inside the lessons (or persisted to CanonicalRoadmap for non-live maps), but for live-КТП subjects the summary is recomputed every read (roadmapLesson.service.ts:47, 134-146). Test generation does NOT reuse lesson content at all — it has its own Test cache keyed by sourceContentHash/dailyPackId.
maps.aiCost: AI cost is incurred per (subject, КТП-topic) lesson the FIRST time any student opens it (or after the source hash changes), NOT per user and NOT per request — the consolidateLessonContent OpenAI call (gpt-4o-mini) runs only on cache miss/stale and the result is shared across all students (nodeLessonContent.service.ts:107-159). Additional recurring cost: the lazy generateLessonSummary call (gpt-4o-mini) runs on read whenever the target lesson has no summary; for live-КТП subjects the summary is never persisted (persistLessonSummary early-returns, roadmapLesson.service.ts:47), so this summary call is paid on essentially every lesson view per user. Lesson chat (chatLessonNode) is per-message per-user. Downstream test generation cost is separate and content comes from raw book text, not the lesson.
problems: Lesson summary is regenerated on EVERY read for live-КТП subjects: isLiveBuiltFromKtp() makes persistLessonSummary early-return (roadmapLesson.service.ts:47), and consolidateLessonContent's summary is only stored in NodeLessonContent.lessons[].summary if the AI returned one — when it didn't, generateLessonSummary fires per request per user with no cache (roadmapLesson.service.ts:134-146). Real recurring cost + latency on the hot lesson-open path.

Lesson content and node test content are two independent pipelines reading the same textbook: lessons via extractTopicText on node.metadata.sources (nodeLessonContent.service.ts:44), tests via resolveBookContentForAI/getBookContent (subjectContent.service.ts:37,43). They can drift — a student studies the AI-consolidated/deduped lesson but is tested on raw book/chapter text selected by bookId/topicFocus string matching (subjectContent.service.ts:26-34), so questions may cover material not in the lesson and vice versa.

generatedBy:'manual' lessons are never invalidated regardless of source changes (sourceHash is ignored for manual rows, nodeLessonContent.service.ts:108) — stale manual content can silently persist after the textbook is edited.

No versioning/audit on NodeLessonContent: regeneration overwrites the single row in place (findOneAndUpdate upsert, nodeLessonContent.service.ts:138); no history, no version number, and the AI model/prompt version is not part of the cache key, so a prompt/model upgrade does NOT invalidate existing cached lessons.

sourceHash hashes full source texts (computeSourceHash, nodeLessonContent.service.ts:50-52) but consolidateLessonContent truncates inputs to MAX_TOTAL=24000 chars (roadmap.ai.service.ts:378-387). Tiny edits in a truncated-away tail still change the hash and force a costly regen that produces identical output.

Cache write failures are swallowed (.catch on the findOneAndUpdate, nodeLessonContent.service.ts:157): a persistent write error (e.g. duplicate-key race on the unique index from two concurrent first-readers) means every request re-pays the OpenAI consolidation with no error surfaced.
Concurrency: two students opening a never-generated node simultaneously both miss the cache and both call consolidateLessonContent (no lock/dedup), doubling AI cost on cold start.

Brittle AI-output parsing: parseJsonObject does indexOf('{')/lastIndexOf('}') slicing (roadmap.ai.service.ts:9-16); malformed/multi-object responses throw and fall back to raw, uncached sources, degrading the lesson silently.

maps.reuse: Questions are NOT regenerated per attempt — there are several layers of reuse, some surprisingly broad: (1) STRONGEST and most surprising: for non-trial generate (auth and guest), test.controller.ts:267-273 / 218-224 calls findLatestTestBySubject(subjectId) — Test.findOne({subjectId}).sort({createdAt:-1}) — and if ANY prior test exists for that subject it is returned verbatim, ignoring bookId, chapterId, content hash, profile, and questionCount. So most repeat requests serve the newest existing subject-level test rather than generating. (2) Content-hash cache in findOrCreateTest (test.controller.ts:117-136): but this cache lookup is ONLY consulted when useCache = !process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY — i.e. cache is checked only when no API key is set. With a key present, it skips the cache check and Test.create()s a new doc each time (though layer (1) usually short-circuits before this). (3) Solo daily packs (line 378-398): exactly one Test per (subject,book,chapter,profile,date) keyed by synthetic dailyPackId, shared across all users for the day. (4) There is NO durable question bank / question pool: questions live only inside Test docs; the only 'bank' is the cache of whole 10-question Test docs keyed by sourceContentHash = sha256(JSON{v,profile,n,text}). De-duplication of question CONTENT is best-effort and prompt-only: User.getAllQuestionHashes (base64 of questionText, scoped subject+book) is passed as previousQuestions and injected into the prompt as 'avoid repeating the meaning of these' (ai.service.ts:121/147) — no semantic check, just an instruction the LLM may ignore. So: fresh generation only on a true cache/subject miss; otherwise reuse of a whole prior test.
maps.aiCost: AI generation cost is incurred at WHOLE-TEST granularity (one gpt-4o-mini chat completion per generated 10-question Test, or N per batch for >10 ENT), NOT per user and NOT per attempt. Cost is shared because generated Tests are persisted and reused: for non-trial flows the newest subject-level test is reused (findLatestTestBySubject), and solo daily packs are generated once per (subject,book,chapter,profile,day) and shared across all users. previousQuestions (per-user history) only changes the prompt, not whether a call happens. The misleadingly-named analyzeAnswers feedback path is a MOCK (template strings, no LLM, ai.service.ts:368-433) so per-result AI feedback currently costs nothing. Grading at submit is deterministic and free. Net: real LLM spend = number of distinct (cache-miss) test generations, amortized across many attempts/users.
problems: BROAD over-reuse bug-smell: test.controller.ts:268-272 (auth) and 219-223 (guest) serve the newest Test for the ENTIRE subject (Test.findOne({subjectId}).sort(createdAt:-1)) on any non-trial request, ignoring bookId/chapterId/profile/questionCount/topicFocus. A learner asking for a chapter-3 regular 30-question test can be handed a stale full-book 10-question ENT test for the same subject. This dwarfs the precise hash cache below it.

Cache is effectively dead when OpenAI is configured: findOrCreateTest only checks the content-hash cache when useCache = !process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY (test.controller.ts:117). In production (key set), every cache MISS at layer (1) creates a brand-new Test doc even for identical content, accumulating duplicate Test documents.

Schema validator hard-codes 10 questions for ALL profiles: Test.model.ts:109-122 rejects any questions array != 10 and runs ENT validateEntQuestion on each. Regular tests with questionCount 1-9 or 11-50 (allowed by ai.service.ts:73-86) would FAIL Mongoose validation on Test.create — latent inconsistency between AI service limits and the persistence model.

'Coverage of the topic' is NOT guaranteed structurally. There is no enumeration of topics/paragraphs/KTP nodes to ensure each is tested. Coverage is purely a prompt nudge: topicFocus 'all 10 questions should focus on topic X, and if not enough material, generate the closest in meaning' (ai.service.ts:100-104) — which actively invites off-source questions. Topic linkage is reverse-engineered after the fact via case-insensitive title match (resolveTopicTitleToId), and unmatched titles are silently dropped (topicId unset), so those questions never count toward topicsToReview or roadmap mastery.

Anti-hallucination is weak: the only grounding is dumping raw concatenated paragraph text into the user message (Subject.getBookContent/getChapterContent join paragraph.content.text). There is NO retrieval, NO citation verification, NO check that questionText/answers actually appear in the source, and NO answer-correctness verification. relatedContent.pages defaults to [1] whenever the model omits or fabricates pages (ai.service.ts:307-309, entQuestion.util.ts:292), so page references are unreliable.

Question-text matching for grading/answer-lookup is by exact questionText string (checkAnswers test.controller.ts:184; testResult byText map). If two questions share identical text, or a client mutates whitespace, the lookup is ambiguous/fails; dedup hashes are also raw base64(questionText) with no normalization (User.model.ts:239) so trivial rephrasings defeat dedup.

Misleading naming / dead-ish code: analyzeAnswers (ai.service.ts:368) is documented as AI feedback and lives in the AI service but is a pure mock with an artificial delay(800); rephraseQuestion (line 441) and isQuestionDuplicate/hashQuestion (line 507-517) appear unused by the generation path. The file header still says 'AI SERVICE (MOCK)' though generateTest is now a real OpenAI call.

No semantic dedup across attempts/batches: previousQuestions and cross-batch 'don't repeat' are prompt-only; nothing prevents the model from re-emitting near-duplicate questions, and the per-user hash scope (subject+book) misses chapter-level repetition nuance.
aiExplanation is silently backfilled with a generic placeholder string when the model omits it (pickAiExplanation, entQuestion.util.ts:271-278), so a 'required' explanation can be content-free.
evidence: Test schema (Test.model.ts:71-104): subjectId ref Subject required index; bookId ObjectId required index; chapterId ObjectId index; questions: [QuestionSchema]; sourceContentHash: String required index; testProfile enum ['regular','ent'] default 'ent'; timestamps; collection 'tests'.

(No 'difficulty' field, no roadmapNode field.)

flows.name: Per-node progress storage & availability (read path: getPersonalSnapshot)
steps: roadmap.service.ts:284 getPersonalSnapshot(userId, subjectId)
resolveCanonical (line 115) builds the canonical node list LIVE from KtpCatalog (KTP topics → nodes via buildKtpCanonicalNodes), falling back to a stored CanonicalRoadmap doc
Load UserRoadmapProgress doc; if missing, upsert a merged default snapshot (line 298-307); if node count or canonicalVersion drift, re-merge (line 309-316)
mergeProgressWithCanonical (line 174) reconciles stored nodes against canonical order, filling defaults via defaultProgress (line 40: mastered:false,bestScore:0,lowScoreFailCount:0)
masteredSet (line 48) = set of nodeIds with p.mastered===true
availabilityForNode (line 60): no prerequisites => 'available'; else 'available' iff EVERY prerequisite nodeId is in masteredSet, else 'locked'
Build per-node view: availability, mastered, bestScore, lowScoreFailCount, knowledgeMapTestBlocked = lowScoreFailCount >= 3 (line 343)
Compute frontier = available && !mastered nodes; rank by in-progress > unlocksCount > lowest bestScore (compareFrontier line 99); top-K become recommendations; first = nextRecommended
progressPercent = round(masteredCount / totalNodes * 100) (line 406)
flows.aiCalls: Optional AI enrichment ONLY if options.includeAiInsights && process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY (line 437). Calls roadmapAIService.enrichPersonalRoadmap with nodesSummary + last-20 testHistory; produces coachSummary, nextStepExplanation, per-node aiHint. Wrapped in try/catch, falls back to base on failure. Recommendation RANKING itself is pure heuristic (no AI).

flows.name: Completing a knowledge-map test → update mastery + unlock next node
steps: Client POST /tests/submit with testId, answers, roadmapNodeId, roadmapSessionId (test.controller.ts:302)
checkAnswers computes resultSummary.scorePercent; testHistory pushed to User (controller line 309-328)
If roadmapNodeId & roadmapSessionId present (line 333): roadmapService.recordTestSubmitted({userId,subjectId,nodeId,scorePercent,sessionId,submittedAt}) (line 335)
recordTestSubmitted (roadmap.service.ts:776) validates nodeId exists in canonical (line 790)
IDEMPOTENCY: if RoadmapAttempt {userId,sessionId} exists (line 793) OR insert hits dup-key 11000 (line 815), return idempotent snapshot, NO progress mutation
Else RoadmapAttempt.create(...) appends the attempt row (line 805)

Recompute node: cur = existing or defaultProgress; bestScore = max(cur.bestScore, scorePercent) (line 833); mastered = cur.mastered || isMasteredByBestScore(bestScore) i.e. bestScore>=70 (line 834); lowScoreFailCount = scorePercent>=80 ? 0 : min(3, prevFail+1) (line 836-839)
Write updated node back into nodes[] and persist via findOneAndUpdate upsert (line 853)

Call getPersonalSnapshot to recompute availability for ALL nodes — a node whose prerequisites just became mastered flips locked→available automatically (no explicit 'unlock node' write). Return updatedNodesDelta + nextRecommended + topRecommendations (line 865-875)
flows.aiCalls: None in the write path. (recordTestSubmitted calls getPersonalSnapshot WITHOUT includeAiInsights, so no LLM call.)

flows.name: Knowledge-test gating (3-strike block + 'Освоил' reset)
steps: Before starting a node test, assertKnowledgeMapTestAllowed (roadmap.service.ts:151) loads merged progress; if node.lowScoreFailCount >= ROADMAP_MAX_KNOWLEDGE_TEST_FAILS_BEFORE_BLOCK (3) it throws 403 (line 167)
Called from POST /tests/generate and POST /tests/solo/start when roadmapNodeId in body (test.controller.ts:266, 366)
Each sub-80% submit increments lowScoreFailCount (capped at 3); a >=80% submit resets it to 0 (recordTestSubmitted line 836)
User can also reset by reading material: acknowledgeMaterialMastery (line 997) sets lowScoreFailCount=0 (the 'Освоил' button)
flows.aiCalls: None.

steps: markLessonComplete (roadmap.service.ts:933) validates lessonId via nodeLessonIds; enforces sequential gating — all earlier lessons by order must be completed (line 958-962)

maps.reuse: Canonical roadmap is REGENERATED live on every read from KtpCatalog via resolveCanonical/buildKtpCanonicalNodes (roadmap.service.ts:115); the stored CanonicalRoadmap doc is only a fallback and is deleted on rebuild (line 1066). 'Version' is a derived timestamp (max(subject.updatedAt, ktp.updatedAt)/1000), not a stored counter. Per-user progress IS persisted and reused: one UserRoadmapProgress doc per (user,subject), reconciled against canonical via mergeProgressWithCanonical each read (defaults filled, drift re-saved when node count or canonicalVersion changes, lines 309-316). Availability/locked-state and recommendations are NOT cached — recomputed every snapshot. RoadmapAttempt provides idempotency caching keyed on UNIQUE {userId, sessionId} so a re-submitted session does not double-mutate mastery. Lesson content (INodeLessonContent) is AI-generated once and cached by sourceHash (regenerated when mapped source texts change). bestScore is a global max, NOT a rolling window (ROADMAP_BEST_SCORE_WINDOW_N is declared but unused — comment at roadmap.rules.ts:22-23).
maps.aiCost: AI cost is NOT incurred in the progress/mastery write path at all — recordTestSubmitted, applyTrialKtpResults, markLessonComplete, getProgressCounts make zero LLM calls. AI cost appears in two adjacent places: (1) getPersonalSnapshot's optional insight layer (roadmap.service.ts:437-468) — gated on includeAiInsights && OPENAI_API_KEY — costs ~one LLM call PER personal-roadmap read PER user (coachSummary + per-node hints over last-20 test history); it is recomputed every request, NOT cached, so cost scales with reads, not nodes. getHomeOverview deliberately calls it with includeAiInsights:false to avoid this. (2) Test question generation and the lazy AI answer-explanation (testResult.service.buildAiExplanation → aiService.analyzeAnswers) — per attempt, generated lazily on first request to /ai-explanation and cached in testHistory.aiFeedback. Results are per-user (not shared); roadmap AI insights are per-user-per-subject-per-request and shared with nobody.
problems: Inconsistent mastery thresholds create confusing semantics: a regular node test is 'mastered' at bestScore>=70 (ROADMAP_MASTERY_THRESHOLD_PERCENT, roadmap.rules.ts:8) but the SAME test counts as a 'fail' (lowScoreFailCount++) unless scorePercent>=80 (ROADMAP_KNOWLEDGE_TEST_PASS_PERCENT, line 17). So a 70-79% score simultaneously marks the node mastered AND increments the fail counter (recordTestSubmitted lines 834-839). A user can be 'mastered' yet eventually blocked from retaking the test.

Whole-array rewrite on every progress mutation: recordTestSubmitted/markLessonComplete/markLessonRead/acknowledgeMaterialMastery all do filter+push then $set the ENTIRE nodes[] array (e.g. roadmap.service.ts:849-863). Not atomic at the element level and contends on the single document — concurrent submits for different nodes can lost-update each other (read-modify-write race) since there is no positional/arrayFilters update or optimistic version check.

recordTestSubmitted has a non-atomic gap between RoadmapAttempt.create (idempotency marker, line 805) and the progress write (line 853): if the process dies between them, the attempt is recorded as 'seen' but mastery is never applied, and the next retry short-circuits as idempotent (line 793) — silently dropping the mastery update.

mergeProgressWithCanonical is called 3+ times per snapshot (lines 299/309/320) and recordTestSubmitted/getPickerSubjects re-run a full getPersonalSnapshot (which itself re-reads + may re-upsert) — repeated normalize+merge work and extra DB round-trips per request.

N+1 / fan-out cost: getPersonalSnapshot does a per-node Test.findOne to resolve testId (enrichPersonalNodeView line 259) — one query per node; getHomeOverview and getPickerSubjects loop getPersonalSnapshot across all learner subjects, multiplying this. getPickerSubjects also re-queries UserRoadmapProgress AFTER getPersonalSnapshot already loaded it (line 702).

ROADMAP_BEST_SCORE_WINDOW_N (roadmap.rules.ts:23) is declared and documented as a rolling-window option but never used — bestScore is a permanent global max, so a node can never lose mastery even if recent performance collapses (no decay).

Mastery granularity is node-only. q.relatedContent.topicId carries finer signal and testResult.service builds per-topic 'topicsToReview', and trial fans topics out to KTP nodes, but none of this finer-grained correctness is persisted as sub-node mastery — it is recomputed/discarded each time.

canonicalVersion stored on the progress doc is a derived timestamp (Math.floor(max(updatedAt)/1000)); any touch of Subject.updatedAt (e.g. rebuildCanonicalFromKtp line 1069, or unrelated edits) bumps the version and forces a progress re-merge/re-save (line 310), causing write amplification on otherwise-unchanged progress.

applyTrialKtpResults and recordTestSubmitted duplicate near-identical merge→update node→reorder→upsert logic (lines 747-770 vs 828-863) — missing a shared 'mutateNodeProgress(nodeId, fn)' helper; the trial path also forgets to reset/handle lowScoreFailCount, diverging behavior.
evidence: NodeProgressSchema (UserRoadmapProgress.model.ts:12): `nodeId: {type:String, required:true}, mastered:{type:Boolean, required:true, default:false}, bestScore:{type:Number, required:true, default:0, min:0, max:100}, lowScoreFailCount:{type:Number, default:0, min:0}, lessons:{type:[LessonProgressSchema], default:undefined}, lessonReadAt:{type:Date}`

UNIQUE keys: UserRoadmapProgress.model.ts:36 `UserRoadmapProgressSchema.index({ userId:1, subjectId:1 }, { unique:true })`; RoadmapAttempt.model.ts:18 `RoadmapAttemptSchema.index({ userId:1, sessionId:1 }, { unique:true })`

Mastery + fail-counter update on test completion (roadmap.service.ts:833-839): `const bestScore = Math.max(cur.bestScore, scorePercent); const mastered = cur.mastered || isMasteredByBestScore(bestScore); const prevFail = cur.lowScoreFailCount ?? 0; const lowScoreFailCount = scorePercent >= ROADMAP_KNOWLEDGE_TEST_PASS_PERCENT ? 0 : Math.min(ROADMAP_MAX_KNOWLEDGE_TEST_FAILS_BEFORE_BLOCK, prevFail + 1);`

Trial mastery application (roadmap.service.ts:754-757): `const bestScore = Math.max(cur.bestScore, r.scorePercent); const mastered = cur.mastered || isMasteredByTrialScore(r.scorePercent) || isMasteredByBestScore(bestScore); const updated = { ...cur, nodeId:r.nodeId, bestScore, mastered };`

Captures DESIGNED behavior per docs, with explicit notes where docs disagree with each other or with the codebase reality recorded in project memory.

POST /subjects/import with { title, description?, books[] } full nested tree
Server validates title, checks duplicate, then Subject.create(...) with whole tree
Returns created subject + stats (books/chapters/topics/paragraphs counts)

steps: Client picks subject → book → chapter (or 'whole book') (ARCHITECTURE.md:130-136)
POST /tests/generate { subjectId, bookId, chapterId?, fullBook? }
Server assembles content text from Subject via getBookContent / getChapterContent
Check cache by sourceContentHash (SHA-256)
On miss: AI service generates questions with options, explanations, references
Save Test (cache); return questions WITHOUT correctOption and aiExplanation
flows.aiCalls: ai.service.ts abstraction over OpenAI. Prompt = content text → 4 multiple-choice questions w/ options, explanations, refs (ARCHITECTURE.md:252-256). No OPENAI_API_KEY → mock generation of 4 placeholder questions. Reuse via Test.sourceContentHash cache; per-user repeat avoidance via generatedQuestionsHash.

steps: Client POST /tests/submit { testId, answers[] } (ARCHITECTURE.md:138-144)
Server scores answers against Test.questions[].correctOption
AI service analyzes mistakes → summary + recommendations (book, chapter, pages)
Result saved to User.testHistory (auth users only)
Return full breakdown: score, aiFeedback, detailedAnswers per question
flows.aiCalls: Second AI call (analysis): answers + correct answers → summary, per-mistake explanation, whereToRead recommendation. Guest submit (/submit-guest) runs same scoring/feedback but does NOT persist.

steps: Guest takes test via /guest/* public APIs, gets teaser result (score + truncated summary) (ARCHITECTURE.md:146-154, NOTION_DOCS.md:655-664)
Client stores { testId, answers[] } in sessionStorage
Guest logs in / registers
After successful login()/verifyEmail(), client auto-calls POST /tests/claim-guest
Server re-scores, builds AI feedback, saves to User.testHistory
Dedup via generatedQuestionsHash; sessionStorage cleared
flows.aiCalls: AI feedback rebuilt at claim time. Note: claim re-runs feedback rather than reusing the guest submit result.

flows.name: Two-stage registration (ARCHITECTURE/NOTION) vs OTP/OAuth (README)
steps: DOCS/ARCH: POST /register → validate → PendingRegistration + email 6-digit code; POST /verify-email → create User → JWT httpOnly cookie; missing SMTP → code printed to console (ARCHITECTURE.md:156-162)
README instead: OTP via WhatsApp/Telegram bots + Google OAuth (README.md:98, 144-148); separate whatsapp-bot (5112) & telegram-bot (5113) services, Google OAuth env vars
flows.aiCalls: None. Listed as a flow because the auth design is described INCONSISTENTLY across docs (email-code in ARCHITECTURE/NOTION vs WhatsApp/Telegram/Google in README).

flows.name: Roadmap-driven learning (DOCUMENTED design, ROADMAP_SPEC + README)
steps: User selects subject; app requests canonical roadmap (GET /api/roadmaps/canonical?subjectId=) — cacheable client-side (ROADMAP_SPEC.md:31-37, 220-224)
App shows roadmap nodes with states; GET /api/roadmaps/personal returns per-node availability/status/metrics + nextRecommended
User picks an available node → generates test → takes it → result

On submit: POST /api/roadmaps/events/test-submitted { userId, subjectId, nodeId, score, sessionId, submittedAt } → record attempt, recompute mastery, unlock dependents, recompute recommendations (ROADMAP_SPEC.md:152-175, 251-257)
Returning user: 'Resume learning' = GET /api/roadmaps/next top-1 recommended node
flows.aiCalls: Spec describes NO AI in roadmap itself ('Roadmap не должен учить сам', ROADMAP_SPEC.md:24-25). README.md:158 adds 'создание canonical roadmap (вручную или через ИИ)' — admin may AI-author the roadmap. Memory reveals code reality: lesson content is AI-CONSOLIDATED per node (roadmapAIService.consolidateLessonContent), a flow not in the spec.

flows.name: Mastery / availability / recommendation computation (DOCUMENTED rules)
steps: Mastery: node mastered if bestScore ≥ threshold; MVP recommended Rule 2 with threshold=70%, N=3 (ROADMAP_SPEC.md:84-103)
Availability: node available iff ALL prerequisites mastered; zero-prereq start nodes always available; OR-groups deferred post-MVP (ROADMAP_SPEC.md:107-124)

Recommendation: frontier = available & (not_started|in_progress); prioritize in_progress, then highest outDegree (UNLOCKS_NEXT_TOPICS), then lowest masteryScore (LOW_MARTERY); return top-1 + top-K (ROADMAP_SPEC.md:128-149)
flows.aiCalls: None — deterministic, explicitly designed to be 'стабильной и объяснимой' (stable & explainable). Reason codes: CONTINUE_IN_PROGRESS, UNLOCKS_NEXT_TOPICS, LOW_MASTERY, PART_OF_MAIN_PATH.

flows.name: Live KTP roadmap build + consolidated lesson (code reality — NOT in docs/)
steps: roadmapService.resolveCanonical builds nodes live from KtpCatalog via buildKtpCanonicalNodes (no stored CanonicalRoadmap doc)
nodeId = ktp:{ktpTopicId}; rebuild-from-ktp only validates + bumps subject.updatedAt as the learner-facing version
roadmapAIService.consolidateLessonContent merges texts of all mapped book topics into 1 consolidated lesson (dedup w/o losing facts; splits into 2-4 lessons if large)
Lesson content cached in NodeLessonContent keyed by subjectId+ktpTopicId, invalidated by sourceHash; resolver nodeLessonContent.service.ts; no OPENAI_API_KEY → raw fallback
Sequential lesson gating; per-lesson progress in UserRoadmapProgress.nodes[].lessons[]
Trial: book topic fanned out to mapped KTP nodes (mapBookTopicToKtpNodeIds, applyTrialKtpResults)
flows.aiCalls: AI consolidation of lesson content per node (real, undocumented). Cached per node by sourceHash. This is the actual lesson-generation + reuse strategy and contradicts the docs, which describe no lessons and a static stored canonical roadmap.

maps.reuse: DOCUMENTED reuse strategy is two-layer, all keyed by content hashing: (1) Test-level cache — generated Test documents are keyed by sourceContentHash (SHA-256 of assembled book/chapter content); a generate request with a matching hash reuses the cached Test instead of re-calling the AI (ARCHITECTURE.md:54,134-135; NOTION_DOCS.md:108). (2) Per-user repeat avoidance — User.testHistory[].generatedQuestionsHash[] (base64) prevents showing the same questions to a user again and dedups guest-claim (ARCHITECTURE.md:46,153). Roadmap canonical is designed to be reused/served identically to all users and client-cacheable (ROADMAP_SPEC.md:7,35). CODE REALITY adds an undocumented lesson-content reuse layer: NodeLessonContent cached per (subjectId, ktpTopicId), invalidated by sourceHash, with raw fallback when no OpenAI key (project memory). The KTP catalog itself is a reuse mechanism: it collapses the same topic appearing in multiple grade books into ONE consolidated roadmap node/lesson, explicitly to remove duplicated learning ('убрать дублирование обучения').
maps.aiCost: Per docs, AI cost is incurred at two points and SHARED across users at the test level: (1) test GENERATION — one OpenAI call per unique sourceContentHash, then cached in Test and reused across all users hitting the same content (so cost is per unique content slice, not per user) (ARCHITECTURE.md:134-135). (2) test ANALYSIS at submit — one OpenAI call PER submission (per attempt, NOT shared; guest submit and auth submit and claim-guest each rebuild feedback) (ARCHITECTURE.md:140-143, 152). No OPENAI_API_KEY → mock generation, zero cost (ARCHITECTURE.md:253-254). The roadmap spec itself incurs NO AI cost (deterministic). CODE REALITY (memory) adds a third cost point absent from docs: AI lesson CONSOLIDATION per roadmap node (roadmapAIService.consolidateLessonContent), cached in NodeLessonContent by sourceHash so cost is per-node-per-content-version and shared across users, with a no-key raw fallback. NOTE: docs do not describe any cost cap, rate-limit, or token budget anywhere.
problems: MAJOR docs-vs-code divergence on roadmap source: ROADMAP_SPEC.md designs a STATIC stored CanonicalRoadmap (versioned JSON, seeded), and README.md:126 lists a CanonicalRoadmap model — but project memory states the canonical roadmap is now built LIVE from a KtpCatalog (buildKtpCanonicalNodes), nodeId changed from 'book:chapter:topic' to 'ktp:{ktpTopicId}', and static JSON seeding was DELETED. The entire ROADMAP_SPEC describes a superseded design.

Content model in docs is incomplete: docs/ describe only Subject→book→chapter→topic→paragraph, but code reality has Subject.subjectKind (main|profile), Subject.Topic.ktpTopicIds[] M:N mapping to KTP, a separate KtpCatalog model, NodeLessonContent, and ProfileSubjectPair — none documented. A reader of docs would not know lessons or KTP exist.

Docs describe NO lessons and NO question bank: the platform per docs only generates TESTS on the fly + caches them by content hash. There is no documented question-bank entity, no knowledge-block/subtopic abstraction beyond paragraph. The real consolidated-lesson layer (NodeLessonContent) and AI lesson consolidation are entirely undocumented, so 'where lessons come from' is invisible in docs.

Auth design is self-contradictory across docs: ARCHITECTURE.md & NOTION_DOCS.md describe email 6-digit code verification (PendingRegistration, SMTP) as the registration path, while README.md describes WhatsApp/Telegram OTP bots + Google OAuth (separate services on 5112/5113, Google OAuth env vars). The two cannot both be the current design without reconciliation.

Roadmap API contracts (/api/roadmaps/*) in ROADMAP_SPEC use a different base path (/api/...) and POST /events/test-submitted event model than the rest of the platform's REST style (/api/v1/...) and never appear in NOTION_DOCS API reference — so the roadmap endpoints are unverified against the documented API surface.

ROADMAP_SPEC openly admits unresolved gaps it labels but does not resolve: canonical VERSIONING with progress ('если canonical поменяется, надо понимать что делать с прогрессом' — ROADMAP_SPEC.md:188-192, 273-275), idempotency by sessionId (269-271), concurrent submits needing transactions/optimistic locking (281-283), guest→auth progress merge (277-279). Memory confirms progress migration was NOT implemented ('Миграции прогресса не делали').
NOTION_DOCS.md role list (admin|user) omits the 'teacher' role that memory says exists in code (admin-lite: book decomposition + KTP mapping). Documented permission model understates the real one.

Embedding-everything decision (Subject as one big nested doc) is justified for whole-content reads (ARCHITECTURE.md:35) but scales poorly: a subject with many grade books + paragraphs becomes a very large single MongoDB document; mutating one paragraph rewrites the whole tree, and the 16MB BSON limit is a latent ceiling the docs never mention.

Hardcoded Russian main-subject titles are a fragile coupling the docs never surface: trial.service.ts hardcodes MAIN_TRIAL_TITLES and matches subjects BY title (memory), so renaming a main subject silently breaks ЕНТ trial generation — an undocumented landmine.

claim-guest and submit-guest re-run the AI analysis rather than reusing the guest's already-computed feedback (ARCHITECTURE.md:146-154), meaning a guest who claims a test pays the analysis AI cost twice (guest submit + claim) for the same answers.
