verdicts.claim: There is NO persistent question bank: every test is generated fresh by the LLM per attempt/request, and previously generated questions are not reused across users or attempts.
evidence: server/src/models/Test.model.ts:71-104 — A persistent Mongoose `Test` model backed by `collection: 'tests'` stores the full generated `questions` array (`questions: [QuestionSchema]`) along with `subjectId`, `bookId`, `chapterId`, `sourceContentHash`, `testProfile` and `timestamps`. This IS a persisted store of generated questions, contradicting 'NO persistent question bank'.

server/src/controllers/test.controller.ts:28-30 — `findLatestTestBySubject(subjectId)` runs `Test.findOne({ subjectId }).sort({ createdAt: -1 })`. The filter is ONLY `subjectId` (no userId), so it returns the newest persisted test for that subject regardless of which user created it.

server/src/controllers/test.controller.ts:267-273 — authenticated `generateTest`: `if (!dto.forTrial) { const cached = await this.findLatestTestBySubject(dto.subjectId); if (cached) { success(res, this.sanitize(cached), 'Test loaded from last saved (same subject)', 201); return; } }` — returns a previously stored test and never calls the LLM. A different user requesting the same subject reuses the first user's generated questions.
server/src/controllers/test.controller.ts:218-224 — `generateTestGuest` has the same `!dto.forTrial` cache-and-return-from-DB behavior, message 'Test loaded from last saved (same subject)'.

server/src/controllers/test.controller.ts:378-398 — `startSoloTest` (daily pack): `let test = await Test.findOne({ subjectId, bookId, chapterId..., testProfile..., sourceContentHash: dailyPackId }).sort({ createdAt: -1 }); if (!test) { ...aiService.generateTest...; test = await Test.create({...sourceContentHash: dailyPackId...}) }`. The `dailyPackId` (lines 32-49) is a deterministic hash of subject|book|chapter|profile|dateKey with NO user component, so all users hitting the same daily pack on the same day reuse one stored Test — explicit cross-user reuse across attempts.

server/src/controllers/test.controller.ts:107-136 — `findOrCreateTest` also performs a content-hash cache lookup: `const cached = await Test.findOne(cacheFilter).sort({ createdAt: -1 }); if (cached) return cached;` keyed on subjectId/bookId/chapterId/sourceContentHash/testProfile (no user). (Gated by `const useCache = !process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY;` at line 117.)

server/src/models/User.model.ts:233-240 — `getAllQuestionHashes(subjectId, bookId)` aggregates `generatedQuestionsHash` across the user's `testHistory`; these are passed to the generator (controller line 277) as `previousQuestions`, and ai.service.ts:121/147 inject them as a soft prompt hint 'Избегай повторения смысла этих вопросов'. This mechanism only exists because prior questions ARE persisted; it is an anti-repetition hint, not proof of always-fresh generation.

server/src/models/Test.model.ts:124-143 — `findCachedTest` static and Test.model.ts:106 compound index `{ subjectId, bookId, chapterId, sourceContentHash, testProfile }` exist specifically to support cache lookups of stored tests.

server/src/services/ai.service.ts:60-69 — `generateTest` does call OpenAI (`openAiJsonCompletion`, lines 452-484, model gpt-4o-mini) ONLY when a generation path is actually reached; but as shown above the controller short-circuits to cached/stored tests before calling it in the common non-trial path.
verdicts.nuance: The claim is partly true only for the narrow `forTrial === true` path: when `dto.forTrial` is true, the controller skips `findLatestTestBySubject` (test.controller.ts:218, 267) and goes straight to `aiService.generateTest`, so trial/probe tests are generated fresh. It is ALSO true that there is no de-duplicated 'item bank' in the classic assessment sense (no reusable individual question pool with item IDs) — reuse happens at the whole-test (10-question pack) granularity, not per-question. And the content-hash cache in `findOrCreateTest` is disabled when `OPENAI_API_KEY` is set (useCache = !apiKey, line 117). HOWEVER, the two dominant production paths — non-trial `generate`/`generate-guest` (reuse newest Test by subject across all users) and `solo/start` daily packs (reuse Test by deterministic per-day dailyPackId across all users) — are NOT gated by the API key and DO persist and reuse generated questions across users and attempts. Therefore the blanket claim ('every test generated fresh per attempt' and 'not reused across users or attempts') is false. Also note ai.service.ts is described as gpt-4o-mini OpenAI, not Anthropic.

verdicts.claim: Lesson content IS cached and reused per roadmap node (shared across users) via NodeLessonContent — it is NOT regenerated per user each time.
evidence: server/src/models/NodeLessonContent.model.ts:25-36 — schema fields are subjectId, ktpTopicId, sourceHash, lessons, generatedBy; NO userId field. Unique index is per subject+topic: `NodeLessonContentSchema.index({ subjectId: 1, ktpTopicId: 1 }, { unique: true })` and collection 'node_lesson_content'.

server/src/models/NodeLessonContent.model.ts:4-8 (comment) — 'Кэш сгенерированного (AI-консолидированного) контента урока узла КТП ... контент урока — генерируется один раз и кэшируется здесь. Инвалидация по sourceHash'.

server/src/services/nodeLessonContent.service.ts:107-110 — cache-read path: `const row = await NodeLessonContent.findOne({ subjectId, ktpTopicId }); if (row && row.lessons?.length && (row.generatedBy === 'manual' || row.sourceHash === hash)) { return storedToLessons(row); }` — returns cached content without regenerating when hash matches or manual.

server/src/services/nodeLessonContent.service.ts:138-157 — on miss/stale, generates via roadmapAIService.consolidateLessonContent then writes back with `NodeLessonContent.findOneAndUpdate({ subjectId, ktpTopicId }, { $set: {...lessons} }, { upsert: true })` — key is subjectId+ktpTopicId only, no user.

server/src/services/nodeLessonContent.service.ts:78-87 — getCachedNodeLessons signature is `(subjectId: string, node)`; queries `NodeLessonContent.findOne({ subjectId, ktpTopicId })`. No userId parameter or filter.

server/src/services/nodeLessonContent.service.ts:93-97 — resolveNodeLessons signature is `(subjectId, subject, node)`; no userId. computeSourceHash (lines 50-52, 105) hashes mapped book-topic texts only, so the key/invalidation are content-driven and user-independent.

server/src/services/roadmapLesson.service.ts:113-114 — caller passes only subjectId: `const node = await this.resolveNode(subjectId, nodeId); const lessons = await resolveNodeLessons(subjectId, subject, node);`. The userId in getLesson (line 102) is used solely for per-user progress (lines 117-122: getNodeProgress / completedSet), not for content.
server/src/types/roadmap.types.ts:59-70 — INodeLessonContent interface: subjectId, ktpTopicId, sourceHash, lessons, generatedBy — no userId.

Grep across server/src confirms callers (roadmapLesson.service.ts:114,183,214 and roadmap.service.ts:946) and there is zero occurrence of userId in nodeLessonContent.service.ts or NodeLessonContent.model.ts. Subject-level deletion at subject.controller.ts:297 `NodeLessonContent.deleteMany({ subjectId: oid })` is also keyed by subject, not user.
verdicts.nuance: The cache is shared per (subjectId, ktpTopicId) pair across ALL users — correct. Caveats a careful architect should know: (1) Caching only happens when OPENAI_API_KEY is set. Without it (nodeLessonContent.service.ts:112-114) and on AI consolidation errors (lines 123-126), the code returns a raw fallback (getNodeLessons(node)) that is explicitly NOT cached ('сырой fallback, не кэшируем'), so in that mode content is recomputed every request — but it is still not user-specific, just uncached. (2) Cache invalidation is content-driven via sourceHash (hash of mapped book-topic texts); changing source texts triggers regeneration once, shared again for everyone. A 'manual' generatedBy row is always treated as a hit regardless of hash. (3) Only the consolidated lesson CONTENT is shared. Per-user state (read progress, completion, locking, lesson selection) is layered separately in roadmapLesson.service.ts using userId and UserRoadmapProgress, and lesson summaries can be AI-generated/persisted separately (lines 134-146) — but none of that contradicts the claim about content caching.

verdicts.claim: The roadmap is built live from the KTP catalog (KtpCatalog) reference, NOT from the linear topic list of books; roadmap nodes are derived from KTP entries.
evidence: roadmap.service.ts:115-131 — resolveCanonical() checks KtpCatalog FIRST: `const ktp = await KtpCatalog.findOne({ subjectId })...; if (ktp && Array.isArray(ktp.topics) && ktp.topics.length > 0) { const ktpNodes = buildKtpCanonicalNodes(subject, ktp); if (ktpNodes.length > 0) { ...; return { version, nodes: ktpNodes, fromChapters: true }; } }`. Only when KTP is absent/empty does it fall back to the stored CanonicalRoadmap doc (lines 133-145).

roadmap.service.ts:110-114 — docstring on resolveCanonical: 'Приоритет: live-сборка из КТП (КТП-справочник + маппинг тем книг). Иначе — сохранённый в Mongo canonical (ручной JSON через upsertCanonicalAdmin).'

roadmapKtp.util.ts:67-72 — node generation iterates KTP topics sorted by order: `const ktpTopics = [...(ktp.topics || [])].sort((a, b) => a.order - b.order); ... for (const kt of ktpTopics) {`. One node is pushed per KTP topic (lines 97-119).

roadmapKtp.util.ts:78,99,101 — each node is derived from the KTP entry: `nodeId = ktpNodeId(ktpTopicIdStr)`, `title: kt.title`, and linear `prerequisites: prevNodeId ? [prevNodeId] : []` (chain ordered by KTP order, not book order).
ktpNode.util.ts:7-11 — nodeId format is `ktp:{ktpTopicId}`, tied to the KTP topic's _id (KTP_NODE_PREFIX = 'ktp:').

roadmapKtp.util.ts:46-65,75-76 — book topics serve only as a reverse index (sourcesByKtp) mapping Subject.Topic.ktpTopicIds onto KTP topics to attach lessons/sources; a KTP topic with zero mapped book sources is SKIPPED: `if (sources.length === 0) continue;`. The book topic list does not define node structure.

roadmap.service.ts:128 / roadmapKtp.util.ts (subject.updatedAt + ktp.updatedAt) — version is computed live from max(subject.updatedAt, ktp.updatedAt), confirming nodes are assembled on-read rather than from a persisted node list.

roadmap.service.ts:191,293,532,665,744,789,882,943,1005 — all roadmap consumers (getCanonical, getPersonalSnapshot, getProgressCounts, getPickerSubjects, recordTestSubmitted, markLessonRead, etc.) route through resolveCanonical, so the KTP-first live build governs the whole feature.
KtpCatalog.model.ts:8-13 — model docstring: 'КТП — канонический упорядоченный список тем... Роудмап строится ИЗ КТП: узел роудмапа = тема КТП... nodeId роудмапа (`ktp:{ktpTopicId}`).'
verdicts.nuance: The KTP-live path is the PRIORITY but not the ONLY source. resolveCanonical (roadmap.service.ts:133-145) falls back to a persisted CanonicalRoadmap document when no KTP catalog exists (or it has no topics, or buildKtpCanonicalNodes yields zero nodes — e.g. no book topic is mapped via ktpTopicIds). That stored doc is populated either by admin manual JSON (upsertCanonicalAdmin, controller:152) or by AI generation from book text (generateCanonicalFromBookContent / generateCanonicalFromBookAdmin, controller:90-138, ai.service.ts:29). Importantly, even this fallback is NOT 'the linear topic list of books' — it is hand-authored or AI-derived graph structure. So the precise statement is: roadmap nodes are never a direct projection of a book's topic ordering; when KTP is configured they are derived live from KTP entries (with book topics only contributing lesson content via ktpTopicIds mapping), and otherwise from a separately-authored canonical graph. Also note rebuildCanonicalFromKtp (roadmap.service.ts:1042-1077) deletes any stored snapshot precisely because the live KTP build takes precedence — it does not persist KTP-derived nodes.

verdicts.claim: A Subject can contain MULTIPLE books, and content is modeled as embedded hierarchy subject>book>chapter>topic>paragraph with NO distinct "subtopic" (подтема) entity between topic and paragraph.
evidence: server/src/models/Subject.model.ts:150 — main SubjectSchema field `books: [BookSchema]` is an array, so a Subject holds MULTIPLE books.
server/src/types/index.ts:67-73 — `interface ISubject { ... books: IBook[]; }` confirms the multi-book array at the type level.
server/src/models/Subject.model.ts:16 — file comment states the chain explicitly: `Содержит: Subject -> Book -> Chapter -> Topic -> Paragraph -> Content`.
server/src/models/Subject.model.ts:98-117 — `BookSchema` has `chapters: [ChapterSchema]` (embedded), no $ref/ObjectId reference, confirming embedded (not referenced) hierarchy.
server/src/models/Subject.model.ts:82-96 — `ChapterSchema` has `topics: [TopicSchema]` embedded.
server/src/models/Subject.model.ts:64-80 — `TopicSchema` has `paragraphs: [ParagraphSchema]` directly; the only fields are title, order, ktpTopicIds, paragraphs — NO subtopic array.

server/src/models/Subject.model.ts:52-62 — `ParagraphSchema` has `order` and `content: ContentSchema`; ParagraphSchema:35-50 — `ContentSchema` holds text/pages/metadata. Paragraph is the leaf above Content.
server/src/types/index.ts:38-46 — `interface ITopic { _id; title; order?; ktpTopicIds?; paragraphs: IParagraph[]; }` — topic points straight to paragraphs, no intermediate entity.

server/src/controllers/subject.controller.ts:223-242 — addParagraph nests under topic via topicId (param) resolving book->chapter->topic->paragraphs.push, mirroring the exact 5-level embedded chain with no subtopic step.

Repo-wide grep for `subtopic|sub-topic|sub_topic|подтема|подтемы|subTopics` over *.ts/*.tsx (excluding node_modules) returned only one hit: server/src/services/roadmap.ai.service.ts:396 — a Russian LLM-prompt string ('раздели на 2–4 последовательных урока по подтемам') instructing lesson splitting, NOT a schema/type entity. No subtopic entity exists in the content model.
verdicts.nuance: Two caveats worth knowing. (1) The multi-book capability is real and exercised: addBook/importBook push onto subject.books (subject.controller.ts:161-188), but in practice methods like resolveBookContentForAI and the schema instance methods (getBookContent/getChapterContent/getTopicsContent, Subject.model.ts:168-212) operate on a single book selected by bookId — multi-book is a storage capability, queries are per-book. (2) The hierarchy actually has SIX embedded levels if you count the leaf: subject>book>chapter>topic>paragraph>content (Content is a sub-schema, Subject.model.ts:35-50). The claim's five-level chain stops at paragraph and is accurate for the structural levels; Content is the payload of a paragraph, not a peer entity. The only token resembling 'subtopic' (подтема) anywhere is inside an AI prompt string for roadmap lesson generation and has no bearing on the content schema between topic and paragraph.

verdicts.claim: Test/question generation is grounded in node/topic content (source text is fed to the LLM) and there is some attempt to ensure topic coverage.
evidence: server/src/services/subjectContent.service.ts:36-46 — resolveBookContentForAI sets contentText via subject.getBookContent(dto.bookId) (full book) or subject.getChapterContent(dto.bookId, resolvedChapterId) (chapter), then assigns it to contentForAI.text (line 57).

server/src/models/Subject.model.ts:168-177 — getBookContent: `return book.chapters.flatMap((chapter) => chapter.topics).flatMap((topic) => topic.paragraphs).map((paragraph) => paragraph.content.text).join('\n\n')` — i.e. real paragraph source text, not mock.
server/src/models/Subject.model.ts:183-194 — getChapterContent joins the chapter's paragraph content.text the same way.

server/src/services/ai.service.ts:153-164 — the prompt is built as `[specBody, '', 'Требования:', languageRule, topicFocusHint, '', 'Контент для генерации:', content.text]` — the extracted source text is appended verbatim into the user message.

server/src/services/ai.service.ts:66-69 — `const apiKey = process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY; if (!apiKey) { throw new Error('OPENAI_API_KEY is not defined...') }` — live generation always calls the real LLM (openAiJsonCompletion -> gpt-4o-mini, lines 452-471); despite the file's 'MOCK' header comment (line 30), generateTest is fully wired to OpenAI.

server/src/services/ai.service.ts:92-94 — topicsList: the chapter/book topic titles are injected as a hint: 'relatedContent.topicTitle — точное название темы из списка: [...]. Если вопрос не привязан к конкретной теме, опусти topicTitle.' (an OPTIONAL label, not a coverage requirement).

server/src/services/ai.service.ts:100-104 — topicFocusHint: a soft directive 'ВАЖНО: Все N вопросов должны быть сфокусированы на теме «...»' — the only coverage-shaping instruction, and only present when topicFocus is set.
server/src/services/subjectContent.service.ts:39,45,56-67 — topics[] (chapter/book topic titles) and topicFocus are gathered into contentForAI.metadata and thereby reach the prompt.

server/src/services/roadmap.ai.service.ts:393-395 — consolidateLessonContent instructs the model 'сохрани ВСЕ различные факты (ничего важного не теряй)' and 'НЕ добавляй фактов, которых нет в источниках', feeding actual source text blocks (sourcesBlock, lines 381-404).

server/src/services/nodeLessonContent.service.ts:35-48,102-122 — sourcesWithText extracts per-topic text via extractTopicText and passes it to consolidateLessonContent; roadmap lessons are grounded in mapped book-topic source text.
server/src/utils/roadmapChapter.util.ts:36-40 — extractTopicText returns the joined paragraph content.text for a topic — real source content.

server/src/utils/entQuestion.util.ts:291-296 — post-generation validation only normalizes relatedContent.pages and an OPTIONAL topicTitle per question; there is NO check that the topic list is covered.

server/src/services/ai.service.ts:256-278 — the only enforcement loop checks question COUNT (n !== expectedN) and per-question structure (parseAndValidateEntQuestions / parseAndValidateRegularQuestions); no per-topic quota or coverage validation exists.

server/src/controllers/test.controller.ts:79-100 resolveTopicTitleToId maps the AI-returned topicTitle back to a topicId, but only when present — reinforcing that topic attribution is best-effort, not enforced.
verdicts.nuance: The first disjunct is true, but with an important asymmetry: grounding is strong and hard (real DB paragraph text is concatenated and injected verbatim into the prompt; generation hard-fails without OPENAI_API_KEY, and the 'MOCK' header comment in ai.service.ts is stale/misleading — the code is fully wired to gpt-4o-mini). Coverage, by contrast, is only a SOFT prompt-level attempt and is NEVER enforced: the topic list is passed as an optional per-question label (topicTitle), and topicFocus is a soft 'focus all N questions on topic X' instruction. There is no post-generation validation that questions span the topics — the only hard checks are question count and per-question structural validity, plus a fixed 10-question type distribution for ENT. So a model could legitimately concentrate all questions on a single topic and pass every validation gate. Also note grounding is bounded by truncation limits in the roadmap paths (MAX_CHARS 16000, MAX_TOTAL 24000) — the test-generation path (ai.service.ts) does NOT truncate content.text, so very large books are sent whole, which could exceed token limits but does not reduce grounding fidelity.
